channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: PAGASA Announces Onset of Southwest Monsoon as Rainy Season Nears
item.description: PAGASA officially declared the start of the southwest monsoon, locally known as habagat, on May 30, 2026. According to the weather bureau, recent weather analyses showed the presence of southwesterly ...

item.title: Trump signals possible easing of Strait of Hormuz blockade amid ongoing Iran-US negotiations
item.description: Donald Trump has stated that a US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz could be lifted as negotiations continue over a potential agreement with Iran aimed at extending a ceasefire and opening the do...

item.title: Full breakdown of the ‘Star City’ soundtrack featuring James Brown, Edita Piekha and more
item.description: Apple TV+’s space race drama ‘For All Mankind’ has expanded with its first spin-off series, ‘Star City’, which explores an alternative history of the Soviet Union’s space programme after it becomes th...

item.title: Investigations underway after two explosive devices detonate at Woolworths stores in Pretoria and Bloemfontein
item.description: Two Woolworths stores in South Africa have been affected by separate explosive incidents within a 24-hour period, prompting heightened security measures and an ongoing investigation by law enforcement...

item.title: Nigeria Daily News Roundup: Minimum Wage Proposal, Political Tensions, Security Debates, and Sports Updates (30 May 2026)
item.description: This report presents a roundup of key Nigerian newspaper headlines for Saturday, 30 May 2026, covering major developments in politics, economy, security, and sports. A major highlight is the Nigeria G...

item.title: Understanding Different Types of Fitness and How They Affect Health
item.description: Fitness is a broad concept that goes beyond simply looking athletic. It encompasses various aspects such as cardiovascular endurance, muscular strength, body composition, and the ability to perform bo...

item.title: Canada Soccer Extends Jesse Marsch’s Contract Despite Unproven Track Record Ahead of World Cup
item.description: Canada Soccer recently extended head coach Jesse Marsch’s contract until after the 2030 World Cup, despite the fact that he has not yet had the opportunity to demonstrate his capabilities on the inter...

item.title: NBA introduces new draft lottery rules to reduce incentives for losing
item.description: The NBA has officially restructured its draft lottery system to tackle the problem of 'tanking,' where teams deliberately lose games to secure higher draft picks. Starting next year, the lottery will ...

item.title: ICE Agent Arrested in Texas for January Minneapolis Shooting Amid Immigration Operation
item.description: Christian Castro, an ICE agent, has been arrested in Harlingen, Texas, on charges related to a January 14 shooting in Minneapolis, Minnesota. The Hennepin County Attorney’s Office charged him with fou...

item.title: B.C. artist repurposes discarded items into unique handmade figures
item.description: In British Columbia, an innovative local artist has found a unique way to breathe new life into discarded materials, transforming them into imaginative and often whimsical characters. The man, whose c...

item.title: South Africa’s crisis of powerlessness, corruption and misplaced anger towards migrants
item.description: This opinion piece reflects on how power and powerlessness shape social behaviour in South Africa, particularly in the context of rising anti-migrant sentiment. The author uses a simple but powerful a...

item.title: Sting suggests decline in manual labour may be influencing modern masculinity and social behaviour
item.description: Sting has suggested that the decline of manual, physically demanding jobs could be contributing to traits often associated with so-called toxic masculinity. In an interview, the musician reflected on ...

item.title: Audit reveals Defence recovered fraud-related debt from employee and identified weaknesses in misconduct investigations
item.description: An audit of the Department of Defence's investigation and misconduct management processes has revealed that a public servant was required to repay approximately $14,800 after being found to have commi...

item.title: FIA penalises officers over misuse of authority in passenger offloading cases
item.description: According to a report presented to the Senate, the Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) has taken disciplinary action against multiple immigration officers for misuse of authority in cases involving the...

item.title: Pakistani freelancers earn over $950m in foreign exchange in 10 months, outperform regional peers
item.description: According to data released by the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP), Pakistani freelancers have generated more than $950 million in foreign exchange during the first 10 months of the fiscal year 2025-26 (1...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal police ready to maintain order amid truck and anti-foreigner protests
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal police have stated they are fully prepared to manage planned protests by truck drivers and anti-foreigner marches over the upcoming weekend. Acting Provincial Commissioner Major General ...

item.title: ABS-CBN Feature Reflects on 2NE1's Lasting Influence on K-pop
item.description: The article highlights the enduring impact of 2NE1, one of the most influential girl groups in K-pop history. Based on the available information, the feature looks back at how the group helped reshape...

item.title: Opposition Parties Criticise Tinubu Administration Over Rising Hardship and Insecurity

item.title: Government to widen audit of CBSE on-screen marking system following concerns over implementation issues
item.description: The Union education ministry is preparing to expand its scrutiny of the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) on-screen marking (OSM) system, moving towards a comprehensive audit of the platform...

item.title: Blue Jays rally from five-run deficit to defeat Orioles 6–5
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays produced a dramatic late comeback to defeat the Baltimore Orioles 6–5 on Friday night after trailing by five runs entering the seventh inning. Toronto had been held to just two h...
